summary: twilio-sdk: typosquat of Twilio with install-time host and CI metadata exfiltration to hardcoded IP
details: Package name 'twilio-sdk' impersonates the legitimate Twilio npm namespace ('twilio', '@twilio/*'). The package has no functional API — index.js contains only 'module.exports = {};' — and exists solely to execute its postinstall beacon. The package.json scripts.postinstall is an inline 'node -e "..."' one-liner that, on every 'npm install', reads os.hostname(), the current working directory, the Node version, and a wide range of CI/repo environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_PROJECT_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL, SYSTEM_TEAMFOUNDATIONCOLLECTIONURI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, npm_config_registry) and transmits them via HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping. The bare-IP plaintext destination is unrelated to Twilio infrastructure, the README's 'No data is collected' claim contradicts the actual behavior, and the try/catch silent-swallow plus the -e inlining are evasion shapes designed to escape casual package.json review. Installing this package leaks installer host identity and the names/URLs of private repos and internal CI servers to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
